mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in retail sales over the prior three monthly observations signals strengthening household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:RSAFS latest available value was 426563 as of one month before 2013-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or if its rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A material rise in unemployment signals deterioration in labor income and recession risk, lowering expected corporate earnings and risk appetite, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE latest available value was 7.3 as of one month before 2013-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero and consumer prices accelerate, investors infer higher tapering and future tightening risk, raising discount-rate uncertainty and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 and CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation rises by more than 1 percentage point versus its prior 6-month pace
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
series:CPIAUCSL
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.08 as of one month before 2013-09-15
fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 233.318 as of two months before 2013-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate and accelerating-inflation trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in housing starts signals stronger construction activity, durable-goods demand, and household balance-sheet confidence, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average HOUST rises by more than 5% versus the prior 3-month average
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:HOUST latest available value was 896 as of two months before 2013-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing-start rebounds have average 60-day S&P 500 returns less than or equal to the unconditional average, or if the hit rate for positive returns is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production over the prior three months signals improving factory output and operating leverage, raising expected earnings for cyclical sectors and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO latest available value was 98.9462 as of two months before 2013-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are not associated with higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative 3-month changes at p <= 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms broad labor-market improvement, supporting consumption and earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average monthly gain exceeds 150 thousand and UNRATE falls over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
series:UNRATE
fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 136133 as of one month before 2013-09-15
fact:UNRATE latest available value was 7.3 as of one month before 2013-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-and-unemployment trigger produces a 60-day forward return hit rate below 55% or a mean 60-day return not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A clear increase in the federal funds rate marks the start of tighter monetary policy, raising discount rates and funding costs, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.08 as of one month before 2013-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS tightening triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered 5-day returns are nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth signals broad demand weakness and lower aggregate earnings growth, causing investors to de-risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 15681.0 as of five months before 2013-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth-below-threshold triggers are followed by average 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional average or positive returns in more than 55% of triggered observations.
